# Git Configuration Reference

| **Regenerate**    | Run the command above to refresh with current settings |

A comprehensive reference of useful git configuration options.
Each entry shows the config key, its current value in your
environment, which scope it's set in (local/global/system),
what it does, and a recommended value.

## Scope Guide

| Scope | File Location | Applies To |
|-------|---------------|------------|
| **local** | `.git/config` in the repository | This repository only |
| **global** | `~/.gitconfig` or `~/.config/git/config` | All your repositories |
| **system** | `/etc/gitconfig` (Linux/macOS) or `C:\Program Files\Git\etc\gitconfig` (Windows) | All users on machine |

**Precedence:** local > global > system.

Set most configs at **global** scope so they apply everywhere,
then override at **local** scope for project-specific needs
(e.g. different email for work repos, project-specific commit template).

### Updating Config Values

```bash
# Read a value (shows effective value from highest-priority scope)
git config <key>

# Read with origin (shows which file defines the value)
git config --show-origin <key>

# Set at different scopes
git config --global <key> <value>    # ~/.gitconfig
git config --local <key> <value>     # .git/config (this repo only)
git config --system <key> <value>    # system-wide (requires admin)

# Remove a value
git config --global --unset <key>

# List all settings with their scopes
git config --list --show-origin
```

## Core

*Fundamental git behavior: line endings, editor, pager, file handling.*

### `core.autocrlf`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `false` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | `true` |

**Description:** Line ending conversion: 'true'=CRLF<>LF (Windows), 'input'=LF on commit only, 'false'=off

**Recommendation:** 'input' on macOS/Linux, 'true' on Windows

### `core.editor`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `code --wait` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Default text editor for commit messages, interactive rebase, and other editing

**Recommendation:** e.g. 'code --wait', 'vim', 'nano'

### `core.pager`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Pager program for git output (log, diff). 'delta' gives syntax-highlighted diffs

**Recommendation:** 'delta' for better diffs, or 'less -FRX'

### `core.excludesfile`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Path to a global gitignore file applied to ALL repositories (editor/OS ignores)

**Recommendation:** '~/.gitignore_global'

### `core.filemode`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `false` |
| **Set in** | local |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Track file permission (executable bit) changes. Windows doesn't support Unix perms

**Recommendation:** 'false' on Windows if you see spurious permission diffs

### `core.longpaths`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Enable paths longer than 260 chars on Windows. Required for deep node_modules

**Recommendation:** 'true' on Windows

### `core.quotepath`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Quote non-ASCII characters in pathnames. Disable to see UTF-8 filenames correctly

**Recommendation:** 'false' to display non-ASCII filenames correctly

### `core.hooksPath`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Custom directory for git hooks. Usually managed by pre-commit

**Recommendation:** Leave unset when using pre-commit

### `core.eol`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Line ending style in working directory: 'lf', 'crlf', or 'native'

**Recommendation:** 'lf' for cross-platform projects, or use .gitattributes

### `core.safecrlf`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Abort/warn if line ending conversion would lose data

**Recommendation:** 'warn' for safety without blocking

### `core.symlinks`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `false` |
| **Set in** | local |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Whether working tree supports symbolic links (auto-detected on clone)

**Recommendation:** Auto-detected; 'false' if symlinks cause issues on Windows

### `core.whitespace`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Which whitespace problems git notices (trailing-space, space-before-tab, etc.)

**Recommendation:** Default is fine for most projects

### `core.ignorecase`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | local |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Whether filenames are case-insensitive (auto-detected on clone). Affects 'git mv'

**Recommendation:** Auto-detected; usually 'true' on Windows/macOS, 'false' on Linux

## Fetch

*How git retrieves objects and refs from remotes.*

### `fetch.prune`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-remove stale remote-tracking branches on every fetch

**Recommendation:** 'true' -- avoids stale branch accumulation

### `fetch.prunetags`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-remove stale remote tags on fetch

**Recommendation:** 'true'

### `fetch.fsckobjects`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Verify integrity of objects received during fetch (catches corruption)

**Recommendation:** 'true' for security

## Pull

*Behavior when pulling changes from a remote branch.*

### `pull.rebase`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Rebase local commits on top of upstream when pulling. Gives linear history

**Recommendation:** 'true' for linear history

### `pull.ff`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Fast-forward behavior on pull: 'only' refuses merge commits, 'true' allows any merge

**Recommendation:** 'only' to prevent accidental merge commits

---

## Push

*Defaults for pushing commits to remotes.*

### `push.default`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `current` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** What to push when no refspec given: 'current'=same-named branch, 'simple'=default

**Recommendation:** 'current' or 'simple'

### `push.autoSetupRemote`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-set upstream tracking on first push (no more --set-upstream). Git 2.37+

**Recommendation:** 'true'

### `push.followTags`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Push annotated tags along with commits automatically

**Recommendation:** 'true' if you use annotated tags for releases

## Merge

*How git handles merges and conflict markers.*

### `merge.ff`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | local |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Fast-forward on merge: 'only'=refuse merge commits, 'false'=always create them

**Recommendation:** 'only' for linear history, 'false' for explicit merge commits

### `merge.conflictstyle`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `zdiff3` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Conflict marker format: 'diff3'/'zdiff3' shows the common ancestor (3-way diff)

**Recommendation:** 'zdiff3' (git 2.35+) or 'diff3'

### `merge.tool`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Default tool for resolving merge conflicts interactively

**Recommendation:** e.g. 'vscode', 'meld', 'vimdiff'

### `merge.log`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Include one-line descriptions of merged commits in merge commit message

**Recommendation:** 'true' for informative merge commits

## Rebase

*Rebase workflow helpers: auto-stash, fixup squashing, stacked refs.*

### `rebase.autostash`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-stash uncommitted changes before rebase, unstash after

**Recommendation:** 'true' -- prevents 'dirty worktree' errors

### `rebase.autoSquash`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-apply fixup!/squash! prefixes in interactive rebase (with --fixup)

**Recommendation:** 'true'

### `rebase.updateRefs`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-update stacked branch refs during rebase. Git 2.38+

**Recommendation:** 'true' for stacked branch workflows

---

## Rerere

*Reuse recorded resolutions for recurring merge conflicts.*

### `rerere.enabled`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `true` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** REuse REcorded Resolution -- remember and replay merge conflict fixes

**Recommendation:** 'true' -- saves time on repeated rebases

*Temporary work storage: stash display and behavior.*

### `stash.showPatch`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Show the patch (diff) in 'git stash show' instead of just a file stat summary

**Recommendation:** 'true' for detailed stash inspection

## Branch & Init

*Branch/tag display, sorting, and defaults for new repositories.*

### `branch.autosetuprebase`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Auto-set pull.rebase for new tracking branches: 'always', 'local', 'remote', 'never'

**Recommendation:** 'always' if you prefer rebasing consistently

### `branch.sort`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `-committerdate` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Default sort for 'git branch': '-committerdate' shows most recent first

**Recommendation:** '-committerdate'

### `init.defaultBranch`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `main` |
| **Set in** | system |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Default branch name for 'git init'

**Recommendation:** 'main'

### `tag.sort`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Sort for 'git tag': 'version:refname' gives natural version ordering (v1.9 < v1.10)

**Recommendation:** 'version:refname'

| Property | Value |

**Recommendation:** Set if using SSH signing for local verification

## Diff

*Diff algorithm, moved-line detection, and rename handling.*

### `diff.algorithm`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `histogram` |
| **Set in** | global |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Diff algorithm: 'histogram' gives better results than default 'myers'

**Recommendation:** 'histogram'

### `diff.colorMoved`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Highlight moved lines in diffs (not just added/deleted). Makes refactoring clearer

**Recommendation:** 'default' or 'zebra'

### `diff.colorMovedWS`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Whitespace handling for moved-line detection

**Recommendation:** 'allow-indentation-change' with colorMoved

### `diff.tool`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Default tool for side-by-side diff comparison

**Recommendation:** e.g. 'vscode', 'meld', 'delta'

### `diff.renameLimit`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Max files for rename detection. Increase if git misses renames in large changesets

**Recommendation:** Increase (e.g. 5000) for large repos

### `diff.submodule`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Submodule diff format: 'log' shows commit summaries, 'short' shows only SHAs

**Recommendation:** 'log' if you use submodules

*Credential storage, SSL, and object integrity verification.*

### `credential.helper`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `manager` |
| **Set in** | system |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** How git stores credentials (passwords/tokens). 'manager' uses OS credential store

**Recommendation:** 'manager' (Windows), 'osxkeychain' (macOS), 'store' (Linux)

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Verify SSL certs for HTTPS. Disabling is a security risk

**Recommendation:** Keep 'true' (default); only disable for self-signed certs

| Property | Value |

| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Verify object integrity during push/pull transfers

**Recommendation:** 'true' for data integrity

**Recommendation:** 'true' for large repos

| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Show hints in 'git status' about staging, unstaging, and conflict resolution

**Recommendation:** 'true' (default) for beginners, 'false' for cleaner output

---

## Protocol

*Git wire protocol version for network communication.*

### `protocol.version`

| Property | Value |
|:---------|:------|
| **Current value** | `(unset)` |
| **Set in** | unset |
| **Recommended scope** | global |
| **Desired value** | _(no change)_ |

**Description:** Git protocol version: v2 is faster (partial clone, faster negotiation). Git 2.26+

**Recommendation:** '2' (default in modern git)

---

## .gitattributes Reference

`.gitattributes` controls per-path settings in your repository.
Unlike `.gitconfig`, these settings are **committed and shared**
with all collaborators. Place this file in the repository root.

### Sample .gitattributes

```text
# ── Line Endings ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Force LF in the repo, let git handle working-tree conversion
* text=auto eol=lf

# Explicitly mark text files (override auto-detection)
*.py    text eol=lf
*.md    text eol=lf
*.yml   text eol=lf
*.yaml  text eol=lf
*.toml  text eol=lf
*.json  text eol=lf
*.html  text eol=lf
*.css   text eol=lf
*.js    text eol=lf
*.sh    text eol=lf

# Windows-specific files that need CRLF
*.bat   text eol=crlf
*.cmd   text eol=crlf
*.ps1   text eol=crlf

# ── Binary Files ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Mark binary files so git doesn't try to diff/merge them
*.png    binary
*.jpg    binary
*.jpeg   binary
*.gif    binary
*.ico    binary
*.woff   binary
*.woff2  binary
*.ttf    binary
*.zip    binary
*.tar.gz binary
*.sqlite3 binary
*.db     binary
*.pdf    binary

# ── Diff Drivers ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Better diffs for specific file types
*.md diff=markdown
*.py diff=python

# ── Export Ignore ────────────────────────────────────────
# Files excluded from 'git archive' exports
.gitattributes   export-ignore
.gitignore       export-ignore
.github/         export-ignore
tests/           export-ignore
docs/            export-ignore
Taskfile.yml     export-ignore
*.code-workspace export-ignore

# ── Linguist (GitHub) ────────────────────────────────────
# Override GitHub's language statistics
docs/*    linguist-documentation
scripts/* linguist-vendored=false
```

### Pattern Syntax

| Pattern | Meaning |
|:--------|:--------|
| `*` | Match all files |
| `*.py` | Match files ending in .py |
| `text` | Treat as text (enable line-ending conversion) |
| `text=auto` | Let git detect text vs binary |
| `binary` | Treat as binary (no newline conversion, no diff) |
| `eol=lf` | Force LF line endings in the repository |
| `eol=crlf` | Force CRLF in the working tree |
| `diff=python` | Use Python-aware diff heuristics |
| `export-ignore` | Exclude from `git archive` exports |
| `linguist-documentation` | Mark as documentation for GitHub stats |

---

## Applying Changes from This File

You can edit the **Desired value** column in any config entry above,
then apply all changes at once:

```bash
python scripts/git_doctor.py --apply-from git-config-reference.md
```

This reads each entry's **Desired value** and applies it via
`git config`. Only entries where you changed `_(no change)_` to
an actual value are applied. The **Recommended scope** determines
whether it's set at `--local` or `--global` scope.

To preview what would change without applying:

```bash
python scripts/git_doctor.py --apply-from git-config-reference.md --dry-run
```
